heading_hierarchy.impact: Makes the H1+H2 prefix specific so search results and previews distinctly identify this article's support sections, reducing collisions with other pages that use generic support headings.

chunk_autonomy.evidence: Heading 'Want more options?' lists subscription, training, security, accessibility, and community links under one H2
chunk_autonomy.action: Add H3 subheadings under the existing 'Want more options?' H2 to group related links (for example: 'Subscription & training', 'Security & accessibility', 'Community support'); if a group serves a distinct user intent, promote it to its own H2
chunk_autonomy.impact: Creates focused sections so search results and answers match specific user needs (training, security, community), improving relevance and reducing semantic dilution

chunk_autonomy.evidence: Section starting 'BizChatSubsequent can retrieve information from:' repeats lines like 'Your IT admin must have Web search turned on for you.' multiple times and lists several source types without subheadings
chunk_autonomy.action: Convert each source label (Web searches, Microsoft 365 work data, Connected data sources, copilot-memory) into an H3 under the existing sources section and consolidate duplicated sentences into a single concise bullet or sentence under each H3
chunk_autonomy.impact: Creates distinct, focused searchable sections for each source so results match user questions about a specific source rather than returning a blended, noisy result

chunk_autonomy.evidence: Procedural steps under 'Control sources in BizChatSubsequent' and 'Review inline citations in BizChatSubsequent' are duplicated (for example, 'In the Message Copilot compose box, select the Sources button.' appears twice)
chunk_autonomy.action: Remove repeated duplicate bullets throughout the procedural sections so each step appears once; if needed, add H3 substeps to give clear break points for longer procedures
chunk_autonomy.impact: Reduces filler and repetition, increasing signal density so step-by-step instructions surface more reliably in search results

entity_normalization.evidence: H2 headings like "## Control sources in BizChatSubsequent" use the name "BizChatSubsequent"
entity_normalization.action: Replace non‑canonical names in all H1/H2 headings with the official product name (for example, "Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat"), and update any heading variants to match exactly.
entity_normalization.impact: Headings are prepended to every search result; using the canonical product name in headings improves exact‑term matches and raises relevance for queries that use the standard product name.

entity_normalization.evidence: Body alternates between "Chat1st", "BizChatSubsequent", "M365Copilot", "copilot", "Copilot Chat", and "Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat"
entity_normalization.action: Establish the canonical name at first mention (e.g., "Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat (also known as Copilot Chat)") then use that canonical form consistently throughout the article; normalize feature identifiers like "copilot-memory" to the canonical prose form after the bridge if appropriate.
entity_normalization.impact: A single, consistent product name in body text concentrates keyword frequency and improves both exact‑match and semantic search signals, making the article more likely to be returned for queries using the official product name.

disambiguation.evidence: List under 'BizChatSubsequent can retrieve information from:' that enumerates Web searches, Microsoft 365 work data, connectors, and copilot-memory (knowledge types)
disambiguation.action: Clarify the word "source" in-place: use "knowledge source" when referring to types (Web, work data, connectors, memory) and "source file" when referring to a specific file; add that parenthetical or phrase at the first occurrence in each section and update repeated references accordingly
disambiguation.impact: Makes search results and answers distinguish between broad knowledge types and individual files, improving relevance when users search for guidance about enabling sources versus viewing or opening a specific source file

disambiguation.evidence: Instruction in 'Review inline citations' that says 'Select the source file to open it in a side pane' while other sections use 'Sources menu' or 'Sources list' to mean toggles or types
disambiguation.action: Replace ambiguous uses in procedural steps with explicit terms (for example, 'Sources menu (toggle knowledge sources)' or 'Sources list (referenced files)') so each instruction clearly indicates which UI element and meaning is intended
disambiguation.impact: Prevents confusion between UI actions and knowledge-type settings, so users and search will match procedural guidance to the correct task (toggling sources vs inspecting a referenced file)

structured_data_utilization.evidence: Heading 'BizChatSubsequent can retrieve information from:' followed by repeated bullets for Web searches, Microsoft 365 work data, Connected data sources, copilot-memory
structured_data_utilization.action: Replace the repeated bulleted source entries with a concise table inside the same section. Use columns: "Source", "Prerequisite/availability", and "Learn-more link". Remove duplicate sentences and keep one canonical prerequisite statement per source.
structured_data_utilization.impact: Each source and its prerequisite will appear as discrete searchable text, making prerequisites and links retrievable even if surrounding prose is split or excluded.

structured_data_utilization.evidence: Section 'Control sources in BizChatSubsequent' contains repeated step lines and duplicated explanations about the Sources menu and toggles
structured_data_utilization.action: Consolidate the repeated procedural lines into a single numbered step list and add a small two-column table summarizing the Sources menu toggles ("Toggle" / "Effect"), including the "Turn off all" behavior.
structured_data_utilization.impact: Clear steps plus a toggle summary create lexical anchors for actions and effects so users and search can find specific controls and outcomes without parsing duplicated prose.

heading_hierarchy.evidence: Missing H1: article begins with an intro paragraph and then uses multiple '##' headings
heading_hierarchy.action: Add a clear H1 that names the entity and broad intent (for example, 'Declarative agents: features and usage') and make the opening paragraph the lead under that H1.
heading_hierarchy.impact: Provides a stable entity context that will prepend to every section, improving keyword and semantic relevance so search results distinguish these chunks from other articles.

heading_hierarchy.evidence: Top-level H2s 'Example' and 'Additional features' are actually specific to the preceding '## Context IQ (CIQ)' section
heading_hierarchy.action: Demote '## Example' and '## Additional features' to subheadings (###) under '## Context IQ (CIQ)' and clarify their scope as CIQ examples/features.
heading_hierarchy.impact: Avoids generic, stand-alone H2s that are ambiguous to search; keeps the H1+H2 prefix discriminative by grouping related content under the CIQ heading.

chunk_autonomy.evidence: Repeated duplicate lines in Image generator, e.g. "Multiple image generation: For each user prompt, the agent generates four images." appears twice
chunk_autonomy.action: Remove duplicated sentences and consolidate each feature description into a single clear bullet or short paragraph
chunk_autonomy.impact: Removes redundant filler so search results contain higher-quality signals and answers rank more accurately

chunk_autonomy.evidence: Image generator lists distinct features (Multiple image generation; Interactive image options; Image modification; Feedback mechanism; Clipboard and sharing) only as bullets under one H2
chunk_autonomy.action: Promote each feature label to a proper subheading (H3/H4) or clearly separate labeled blocks so each feature serves one intent
chunk_autonomy.impact: Creates clearer boundaries for search results so queries for a specific feature return focused, directly relevant content

context_completeness.evidence: The Image generation capability must be enabled to get the results you want.
context_completeness.action: State inline how to enable the image generation capability or link directly to the settings/permission page and list any required role or feature flag; if enablement differs by tenant or admin setting, state that too.
context_completeness.impact: Prevents users from retrieving an isolated section that says "must be enabled" without knowing where or how to enable it, reducing failed attempts and support questions.

context_completeness.evidence: When you want to reference a document, person, email or meeting in your prompt, simply type a forward slash (/)
context_completeness.action: Add an explicit local qualifier that says which product or UI this applies to (for example, "in Microsoft 365 Copilot" or the Agent conversation window), and state any sign-in or tenant/permission requirements for referencing files (OneDrive for Business access, same work account, etc.).
context_completeness.impact: Ensures the CIQ instructions work when retrieved alone by clarifying the exact app/context and required account or file access, so users don't try the action in the wrong interface or without needed permissions.

disambiguation.evidence: You can copy the generated images to their clipboard to paste into other applications
disambiguation.action: Replace the ambiguous pronoun with an explicit owner, e.g. change "their clipboard" to "your clipboard" or "the user's clipboard" in the "Clipboard and sharing" bullet.
disambiguation.impact: Makes the intended referent explicit so searches for copying images or clipboard actions match this content more reliably and reduce confusion in retrieved results.

heading_hierarchy.evidence: No H1 present; top title appears as '## Introducing Microsoft 365 Copilot app' (H2)
heading_hierarchy.action: Promote 'Introducing Microsoft 365 Copilot app' to the H1 and use H2s for true subtopics under that H1
heading_hierarchy.impact: Provides a clear entity+intent prefix for every search result, improving keyword matches and making results for this article distinct from others

heading_hierarchy.evidence: Generic H2s: '## Help' and '## Learn more' do not narrow the topic
heading_hierarchy.action: Rewrite those H2s to be discriminative (for example, 'Help for Microsoft 365 Copilot app' and 'Learn more about Microsoft 365 Copilot app features'), or add a specific subtopic token to each H2
heading_hierarchy.impact: Reduces ambiguous headings that collide with many other articles, so the H1+H2 prefix better distinguishes these sections in search and ranking

chunk_autonomy.evidence: H2 'Learn more' lists multiple distinct topics in one section: Find files; Design a poster; Gather content; Get started with Chat
chunk_autonomy.action: Convert each item under the 'Learn more' H2 into its own H3 (for example, 'Find files, messages, and people') and add a short explanatory sentence or link beneath each H3.
chunk_autonomy.impact: Separates distinct user intents so search can return focused sections for each task, improving result relevance and answer precision.

disambiguation.evidence: List items 'Find files, messages, and people' and 'Get started with Chat' under 'Introducing Microsoft 365 Copilot app'
disambiguation.action: Clarify ambiguous terms: replace 'Chat' with 'Copilot Chat' (or specify the chat product) and add brief parenthetical scopes for 'messages' and 'people' (e.g., 'messages (email or Teams)', 'people (directory contacts)').
disambiguation.impact: Improves relevance for queries about Copilot Chat and specific message/people types, and prevents mixed interpretations that weaken search and answer matching.

heading_hierarchy.evidence: ## Help; ## Introducing Microsoft 365 Copilot app; ## Learn more
heading_hierarchy.action: Promote "Introducing Microsoft 365 Copilot app" to the H1 and rewrite the other H2s to include the product and a specific subtopic (for example, change "Help" → "Help for Microsoft 365 Copilot app" and "Learn more" → "Learn more about Microsoft 365 Copilot app" or split into specific items like "Find files, messages, and people in Microsoft 365 Copilot app").
heading_hierarchy.impact: Ensures the H1 establishes the entity and intent while H2s narrow to discriminative subtopics, making search results and answers more specific and reducing ambiguous, repeated headings across sibling articles.

chunk_autonomy.evidence: H2 'Learn more' lists multiple distinct tasks: 'Find files, messages, and people'; 'Design a poster quickly'; 'Gather relevant content together'; 'Get started with Chat'
chunk_autonomy.action: Add explicit subheadings (H3) under the 'Learn more' H2 for each listed task and move the corresponding guidance or links beneath each subheading; if a task is a large, standalone walkthrough, promote it to its own H2.
chunk_autonomy.impact: Each task becomes a focused section that returns more relevant search results for specific user queries instead of a single blended result covering multiple intents.

disambiguation.evidence: List item under 'Learn more' reads 'Get started with Chat' — 'Chat' could mean Copilot's chat feature, Teams chat, or a generic chat function
disambiguation.action: Clarify the meaning of 'Chat' in the 'Learn more' list item by naming the specific feature or product (for example, 'Get started with Copilot chat' or 'Get started with Teams chat') and add a parenthetical qualifier on first use if needed
disambiguation.impact: Removes ambiguity so search queries for a specific chat feature match this article more accurately, improving result relevance and reducing mixed-answer returns

context_completeness.evidence: Opening sentence uses the phrase 'Disabling Copilot as described here' (vague backward-reference).
context_completeness.action: Replace the vague phrase 'as described here' with an explicit inline reference or the linked article title; state the SKU restriction directly in the sentence (for example, 'The steps in this article apply only to Microsoft 365 Home and Family SKUs').
context_completeness.impact: Removes a dependency on an external or distant reference so the chunk clearly states the eligibility restriction when it appears alone in search results.

structured_data_utilization.evidence: Instructions target "Windows or Mac" (heading: "To disable Copilot in Word or PowerPoint on your Windows or Mac device") but list no app or OS version requirements.
structured_data_utilization.action: Add a concise "Supported platforms and versions" bullet list immediately before the step-by-step instructions that names the supported Office app names (Word, PowerPoint), minimum app builds or Office versions, and minimum Windows/macOS versions and SKUs (Microsoft 365 Home/Family). Also add brief version notes to each platform-specific step when UI paths differ by version.
structured_data_utilization.impact: Search results will surface platform- and version-specific prerequisites as plain text, making the correct instructions easier to find and reducing retrieval errors when UI or menu paths vary by OS or app version.
